summary: Drew DeVault shares his August 2019 progress update from Osaka, Japan, covering developments across his major projects. SourceHut gained SSH access to build environments for debugging failed builds. aerc 0.2.0 was released with nearly 200 changes from 34 contributors, adding maildir support, search, and filtering. Sway 1.2-rc1 brought features from i3 4.17, and his DRM leasing work achieved VR functionality with SteamVR on Wayland. He also notes progress on his Wayland book, now about half complete.
key_points: SourceHut builds now support SSH access for debugging failed builds and on-demand shells, including experimental arm64 support
aerc 0.2.0 shipped with nearly 200 changes from 34 contributors, and Greg Kroah-Hartman wrote up his email workflow to help guide aerc's direction
DRM leasing for VR on Wayland is now working with SteamVR, with plans to contribute to Monado and OpenXR for a full free software VR stack
Sway 1.2-rc1 and wlroots updates add direct scanout support and features from i3 4.17
money_quotes: You can now SSH into a failed build to examine the failure and investigate the root cause.
aerc 0.2.0 has been released, which included nearly 200 changes from 34 contributors.
Greg Kroah-Hartman of Linux kernel fame was kind enough to write up details about his email workflow to help guide the direction of aerc.
I'm happy to share that I've got it working with SteamVR!
All of the unknowns have been discovered and answered. We will have VR on Wayland soon.
I plan on making my way to the Monado and OpenXR to help realize a top-to-bottom free software VR stack designed with Wayland in mind.
key_insight: DeVault's projects are converging on a vision of fully free software infrastructure — from email clients guided by kernel maintainer workflows, to a complete open-source VR stack built on Wayland.
